summary: Iteration 63 — Frontend validation of Call History sidebar (P1) at /calls. Logged in as bob@japap.com, exercised full UX flow on mobile viewport (390x844). All 10 acceptance criteria pass: page loads with data-testid='call-history-page', header back/refresh buttons present, 6 call rows rendered with testid='call-row-{id}', missed calls show red name color (#FCA5A5) + 'Manqué' label + PhoneX icon, video call row shows VideoCamera badge + VideoCamera callback icon (visible on row with 2:05 duration), audio rows show Phone callback icon, Callback button triggers POST /api/calls/session via CallContext.startCall (then auto-leaves with toast 'Le contact est hors ligne.' since Carol is offline — expected behavior, not a bug), refresh button re-fetches list, back button navigates to /feed, ProfilePage 'Historique appels' link-calls navigates to /calls. French labels all present ('Aujourd''hui', 'Sortant', 'Manqué', 'Historique', 'Vos appels récents'). No JS console errors on /calls. No summary badges rendered (expected — no call_sessions with summaries seeded).

integration_issues.issue: POST /api/calls/session succeeds and CallContext.startCall fires, but the calling/outgoing overlay is dismissed almost immediately because peer (Carol) is offline → backend auto-emits 'leave' and frontend shows toast 'Le contact est hors ligne.' This is expected product behavior (graceful offline handling), NOT a bug. Worth documenting: in-app calling-state UI was not visible long enough to be asserted in DOM (selector-based check returned null). Only the French text 'Sortant' was momentarily detected. To assert the calling overlay deterministically, seed a 2nd online user or mock peer presence as online.

critical_code_review_comments: CallHistoryPage.js (315 lines): clean, well-structured, mobile-first. No splitting required.
fetchHistory has no AbortController — if user spams refresh, races may leak setItems on stale responses. Low priority.

Date grouping uses local timezone via new Date(now.getFullYear()...) which is fine for mobile-first single-region use, but UTC vs local boundary calls near midnight could land in wrong bucket. Acceptable.
ReshareSummaryModal.jsx: solid implementation, AbortSignal via `alive` flag is correct. No issues.
test_credentials: bob@japap.com / Test1234! (login via username field — placeholder 'Nom utilisateur' but accepts email; type=email)
seed_data_creation: None — used existing Bob↔Carol call history (6 calls: 4 outgoing audio ended, 1 outgoing video ended, 2 missed). NB: review_request mentioned 7 calls but endpoint returned 6 (likely dedup of legacy+LiveKit sessions); not a frontend issue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Call History P1 frontend is GREEN. To deeply test (a) summary modal flow (call-row-summary-{id}) and (b) reshare modal (call-row-reshare-{id} → ReshareSummaryModal), seed at least one call_session with has_summary=true for Bob. To assert the persistent outgoing-call overlay after callback, ensure peer (Carol) is online via a 2nd browser context or mock presence. Login form first input has placeholder 'Nom utilisateur' but accepts email — use input[data-testid=login-email-input].
